---
title: "Quarto 6 — Parameters Demonstration"
subtitle: "One trusted source, several controlled variants"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goal — 25 minutes

Understand when parameters reduce duplication, then change one parameter and observe the result.

::: {.fragment}
This is a demonstration, not a requirement for minimum workshop success.
:::

# The problem {.section-divider}

## Same analysis, different case

::: {.incremental}
- PFOA or PFOS
- Three simulated monitoring sites
- Identical table and figure logic
- Different title, filter, and teaching threshold
:::

::: {.fragment}
Avoid six copied files that can drift apart.
:::

# Define parameters {.section-divider}

## In YAML

```yaml
params:
  chemical: "PFOA"
  threshold: 4.0
  site: "Research Triangle Park"
```

## Use them in code and prose

```r
selected <- dat |>
  dplyr::filter(
    params$site == "All" | site == params$site
  )
```

```markdown
This report summarizes **`r knitr::inline_expr('params$chemical')`**.
```

# Dynamic output {.section-divider}

## Captions can use parameter values

```yaml
#| fig-cap: !expr paste(
#|   "Distribution of", params$chemical,
#|   "at", params$site
#| )
```

::: {.fragment}
Validate allowed parameter values before using them to select columns or files.
:::

# Re-render {.section-divider}

## One controlled variant

```r
quarto::quarto_render(
  "afternoon_quarto/06_parameterized_reports.qmd",
  execute_params = list(
    chemical = "PFOS",
    threshold = 6.0,
    site = "Durham"
  ),
  output_file = "report_PFOS_Durham.html"
)
```

::: {.fragment}
Batch rendering is bonus material. Start by proving one variant works.
:::

# Scientific boundary {.section-divider}

## Thresholds are teaching inputs

The data and thresholds in this demonstration are synthetic and arbitrary. They do not represent a regulatory or clinical decision rule.

# Learner edit {.section-divider}

## Change one value

1. Open `student_work/parameter_demo.qmd`.
2. Change one parameter: chemical, site, or threshold.
3. Predict which prose, caption, axis label, and threshold line should change.
4. Render once and compare the result with your prediction.

::: {.callout-important}
Next: return to `student_work/my_report.qmd` for the cumulative capstone.
:::
